hero.title:
Every single time.
AI almost nails it!
hero.subtitle:
AI gets closer every day. Still misses.
5%, 10%, 20% off — across pages, features, prompts.
Global coherence? Forget it. You finish by hand.

The WAI WAY fixes that. Free. Any codebase.

method.title: The Problem with agents
method.subtitle: AI is fast. But fast and wrong gets expensive.
items: AI forgets what it built two prompts ago
No spec means no accountability
You end up debugging AI's shortcuts
"Just prompt it" doesn't scale past a prototype

tooling.title: The Fix "with AI WAY"
tooling.subtitle: A five phase method that keeps your AI focused.
items: Every phase produces a reviewable artifact
You approve before the AI moves on
Works with any codebase and any AI agent
Spec, scaffold, build, test, deploy. In order

0.title: Brainstorm
0.subtitle: Idea to complete spec.
0.outcome: A full spec before a single line of code.
items: Describe what you want in plain language
AI asks questions until nothing is ambiguous
Data models, journeys, permissions, edge cases
Output: spec documents you own and control

1.title: Scaffold
1.subtitle: Clickable app, zero logic.
1.outcome: Navigate the real app before writing business code.
items: Every screen gets a route and a placeholder
Navigation wired end to end
Catch structural mistakes before they cost you
Demo ready in minutes

2.title: Entities
2.subtitle: Your data, locked down.
items: Fields, types, validations. Straight from the spec
State machines with explicit transitions
Who can do what, when. Decided upfront
Single source of truth for the entire app

3.title: Compose
3.subtitle: Real pages, real data.
3.outcome: Every screen does what the spec says.
items: CRUD wired to your entities
AI follows the spec step by step
No freestyling, no invented features
Functionality first, polish later

4.title: Harden
4.subtitle: Ship it.
items: Tests generated from your spec
Security rules from your access definitions
Code review and vulnerability audit
i18n, CI/CD, deploy

cta.title: Follow the WAI WAY.
cta.subtitle: The method is embedded in the framework. Skills and templates are on GitHub.
